claim_tested: Is Akt1 core MF catalytic kinase activity?
key_finding: RAC-alpha serine/threonine-protein kinase; EC 2.7.11.1; Ser/Thr phosphotransfer catalytic activity; kinase domain PF00069, AGC C-term PF00433, PH domain PF00169
confidence_limitations: High; database-level reflecting extensive primary literature

claim_tested: Does Akt inhibit GSK3 by binding or by phosphorylation?
key_finding: GSK3 'is inhibited by serine phosphorylation'; PKB/Akt 'inactivates GSK3' downstream of PI3K
confidence_limitations: High; source reference for the GO:0030291 annotation

key_finding: Rat: IPI ECO:0000353 BHF-UCL ref PMID:8524413 withFrom GSK3a/b; Human: ISS carry-over from rat
Both rat GSK3 (alpha/beta), Ser/Thr kinases EC 2.7.11.26 -- inhibited entities are kinases inhibited by phosphorylation
'Binds to and stops, prevents or reduces the activity of a protein serine/threonine kinase' -- binding-based inhibitor; term set includes true stoichiometric inhibitors e.g. Kip1/p27

claim_tested: Is Akt/PKB a catalytic AGC kinase?
key_finding: PKB/Akt is one of 63 AGC serine/threonine protein kinases activated downstream of growth-factor signaling

claim_tested: Is PKBalpha activated as a catalytic kinase?
key_finding: PKBalpha uses activation-loop + hydrophobic-motif phosphorylation to stimulate its own catalytic activity
